originalTitle: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
title: Brave Helpers Fight a Tiny Germ in Congo!
content:
Hello, Friends from Across Africa!

Hi everyone! Your friendly African news reporter is here! Today, we're going to talk about something important happening in a beautiful country in Africa called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. Sometimes, tiny, tiny germs can make people very sick. There's a special kind of germ called Ebola that is making some people unwell in DR Congo. But guess what? There are many brave doctors and nurses, and big helpers, who are working very, very hard to make everyone better and keep everyone safe! It's like a big team of superheroes fighting against a tiny, invisible enemy. We want to tell you all about it, so you can understand and feel good knowing there are people always helping. They are working hard to make sure everyone is okay.

What is Ebola?

Ebola is a type of sickness that comes from a super tiny germ. We call these germs 'viruses'. You can't see them with your eyes because they are so small! When these tiny germs get inside a person's body, they can make them feel very sick. People might get a fever, which means their body gets very hot. They might feel tired, have a tummy ache, or just not feel like playing. It’s like when you get a cold or flu, but Ebola can be much, much stronger. It’s important to know that it’s not easy to catch Ebola. It only spreads when people touch the body fluids of someone who is already very sick with Ebola. That means things like sweat, tears, or blood. This is why doctors wear special suits to protect themselves! **Did you know?** The first time doctors found out about Ebola was way back in 1976. It was named after a river called the Ebola River in Africa!

Where is DR Congo?

The Democratic Republic of Congo is a very, very big country right in the middle of Africa. It has lots of amazing forests, beautiful rivers, and many different animals. It's home to millions of wonderful people, just like you! It's one of the biggest countries in Africa. Imagine a map of Africa – DR Congo is right there in the heart of it! It’s important to remember that this sickness is only in some small parts of DR Congo, not everywhere. The rest of the country is still bustling with life and joy, with kids going to school and playing games. **Did you know?** DR Congo is so big, it’s like putting four countries the size of France together! It has one of the largest rainforests in the world, after the Amazon.

Super Helpers: MSF and WHO!

Now, let's talk about the super helpers! There are two very important groups that are working hard in DR Congo. One group is called **MSF**. This stands for "Médecins Sans Frontières." That's a big name, but it just means "Doctors Without Borders." Imagine doctors who don't stay in one country but travel all over the world to help people who are sick or hurt, no matter where they are! MSF has many brave doctors, nurses, and other helpers. They go to places where people really need medical care, like in DR Congo right now. They build special clinics and hospitals just for people with Ebola, so they can get the best care. They are very busy helping many people. The other super helper group is the **WHO**. This stands for "World Health Organization." The WHO is like a big team of health experts from all over the world. They work together to keep everyone on Earth healthy! Their leader, a very important person, has just gone to visit the places in DR Congo where people are most sick. When the leader of WHO visits, it means they are taking this very seriously and are bringing lots of expert knowledge and help. They tell everyone the best ways to stop the sickness from spreading and how to help people get better. They are like the coaches for healthy living for the whole world! They want everyone to be strong and well.

Why Are the Helpers Worried?

The doctors from MSF said that the spread of Ebola in DR Congo is "deeply alarming." What does "deeply alarming" mean? It means they are very, very worried. They are worried because the tiny germ, Ebola, is spreading to more people than they hoped. It's like a game of 'tag' where the germ is 'it,' and it's tagging too many people too quickly. When the germ spreads fast, it means more people can get sick, and it becomes harder for the doctors to help everyone. The leader of the WHO, a kind doctor named Dr. Tedros, went to DR Congo himself to see what was happening. This shows just how serious it is, and how much they want to help stop the sickness. They are working extra hard to make sure fewer people get sick and that those who are sick get the medicine and care they need. Their goal is to make everyone healthy again. **Did you know?** Doctors Without Borders (MSF) won a very special award called the Nobel Peace Prize because they do so much good work helping people in difficult places around the world!

How Do the Helpers Stop the Germ?

The brave doctors and nurses are doing many things to stop the tiny Ebola germ. 1. **Finding Sick People:** They go from village to village to find anyone who feels sick. This way, they can help them quickly and make sure the germ doesn't spread to others. They talk to families and friends to learn who might need help. 2. **Special Care:** If someone is sick with Ebola, they take them to a special clinic where they can get medicine and careful attention. These clinics are built to keep the germ from spreading to other people. The doctors and nurses wear special suits, gloves, and masks. It might look a bit scary, but it's just to keep them safe while they help. They are very careful and kind. 3. **Teaching About Cleanliness:** They teach everyone how important it is to wash hands with soap and water, especially after touching things and before eating. Clean hands are super important for fighting germs! They show people the best way to wash their hands, making sure all the tiny germs go away. 4. **Special Vaccinations:** Sometimes, there is a special medicine called a vaccine that can help protect people from getting Ebola. The helpers are giving this vaccine to people who are most at risk, like doctors and nurses, and people who have been near someone with Ebola. It's like a shield for your body! It helps your body fight off the sickness before you even get it. **Did you know?** Handwashing with soap and water can remove millions of germs from your hands! It’s one of the best ways to stay healthy.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still do important things! 1. **Be Kind:** We can remember the people in DR Congo and send them kind thoughts. Everyone needs kindness, especially when things are tough. Thinking about others is a wonderful thing to do. 2. **Learn More:** Keep learning about how to stay healthy. Wash your hands often, eat good food, and play outside! When you know how to stay healthy, you help everyone around you. You become a little health helper yourself! 3. **Appreciate Our Health Heroes:** Remember the amazing doctors, nurses, and health workers who help people every day. They are real-life superheroes! We can be thankful for all their hard work and bravery. So, even though there's a serious problem with a tiny germ in DR Congo, remember that there are lots of amazing, brave people working tirelessly to help. They are doing everything they can to make sure people get better and to stop the spread of Ebola. We are all part of one big African family, and together, we can learn, stay safe, and support each other. You are all amazing, and remember to always stay curious and healthy!
summary: Brave doctors and big health groups are working super hard in DR Congo to stop a sickness called Ebola from spreading and to help people get better.
whyMatters: This matters because tiny sickness germs can make people very sick, but knowing about them and having super helpers means we can fight them and keep everyone safe and healthy across Africa and the world.

title: 'Gifts' from a lover and 'botched' cocaine raids: Police inquiry grips South Africa

originalTitle: PSG beat Arsenal to win back-to-back Champions League titles after shootout
title: PSG beat Arsenal to win back-to-back Champions League titles after shootout
content: PSG win back-to-back Champions League titles with 4-3 shoot-out win over Arsenal following a 1-1 draw after extra time.

originalTitle: Women bear the brunt of DRC’s Ebola outbreak
title: Brave Women and Super Doctors Fighting a Sneaky Germ in Congo!
content:
Hello, awesome friends! Reporter Kiki here, bringing you super important news from a beautiful country in Africa called DRC. It's like our neighbour, full of friendly people! Today, we're talking about a special kind of superhero: the women of DRC. They are super brave and strong, especially when it comes to fighting a sneaky germ called Ebola.

What is the Sneaky Germ Called Ebola?

Imagine a tiny, tiny monster that you cannot see! That monster is like a germ. Most germs are not very scary, and they just give us a little cough or sniffle. But Ebola is a special kind of germ that can make people very, very sick. It can make you feel tired, have a fever, and have a tummy ache. It's not like a regular cold; it's much more serious. Doctors and nurses are super smart people who know how to fight these germs. They wear special suits and use special medicines to help people get better. They work very hard to keep everyone safe and stop the germ from spreading.

Where is DRC? It's a Friend in Africa!

DRC stands for the Democratic Republic of Congo. It's a big, beautiful country right here in Africa! It has amazing animals, tall trees, and friendly people, just like where you live. Sometimes, bad things happen in all parts of the world, even in lovely places like DRC. And right now, the people in DRC are working very hard to stop the Ebola germ.

Our Mums and Grandmas are Super Caregivers!

In many families, it's usually the mums, grandmas, and big sisters who take care of everyone. If someone gets a little sick, who brings them a warm blanket or a yummy soup? Often, it's a woman! This is called being a "caregiver." When someone in a family gets the Ebola germ, these amazing women step up to help. They want to make their family members feel better. But because the germ can spread easily when people are close, it means these brave women sometimes get sick too. They are trying to help, and that makes them very brave, but it also means they face more danger. Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water for 20 seconds can wash away many sneaky germs! That's like singing "Happy Birthday" twice!

Our Nurses and Doctors are Women Superheroes Too!

Many of the kind doctors and nurses who work in hospitals and clinics are women. They wear special suits that look a bit like astronaut suits, so they don't catch the germ. They give medicine, make people comfortable, and work day and night to fight the Ebola germ. Imagine being so brave that you go to work every day to help people who are very sick, knowing that the germ is tricky. That's what these women do! They are true superheroes, helping to heal their community.

Strong Women Keep Families Going

When someone gets sick, it's not just about feeling unwell. Sometimes, the family might not have enough money if the person who earns it is sick. In DRC, many women work hard to earn money for their families. They might sell fruits in the market, make beautiful crafts, or grow food in the fields. If a mother or grandmother gets sick with Ebola, it makes things very hard for the children. They might not have anyone to cook for them or help them with school. So, keeping women healthy is super important for keeping all the families in DRC strong and happy. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, women are the main people who grow food for their families! They are amazing farmers!

Learning and Sharing to Stay Safe

The women in DRC are also very good at helping everyone learn how to stay safe. They teach their children and neighbours about washing hands, and what to do if someone feels sick. They help make sure everyone understands how to stop the germ from spreading. It's like when your teacher tells you to cover your mouth when you cough – they are helping you keep everyone safe! These women are like community teachers, making sure everyone knows the rules to beat the Ebola germ.

Super Survivors Are Our Stars!

Even though Ebola is a very serious sickness, many people get better! These people are called "survivors." Many women in DRC have fought the Ebola germ and won! They are super strong and brave. When they get better, they often help others who are sick or scared. They can tell their stories and show everyone that it is possible to beat the germ. They give hope to their communities, like a bright light in the dark. They are truly shining stars! Did you know? When you get better from a sickness, your body learns how to fight that germ next time! It's like your body has a superhero memory!

Everyone is Helping!

It's not just the women, of course! Men, boys, and girls, and people from all over the world are helping the people in DRC. Doctors, scientists, and helpers from different countries are working together like a giant, super team. They are finding new ways to fight the germ, like special medicines and vaccines (which are like shields for your body!). This big team effort shows us that when we work together, we can overcome big challenges. It's like when you and your friends work together to build the tallest sandcastle – teamwork makes everything better!

Let's Be Kind and Learn More!

We can all help by learning about places like DRC and understanding how people there are working hard. We can be kind to everyone, no matter where they are from. And we can remember the brave women who are doing so much to keep their families and communities safe from the sneaky Ebola germ. They are truly heroes without capes, working every day to make their world a healthier, happier place. Let's send them our big cheers and positive thoughts!
summary: Brave women in DRC are working extra hard as caregivers and healthcare heroes to fight the tricky Ebola germ and keep their families and communities safe and healthy.
whyMatters: It's important to know that women are doing so much brave work to help their communities stay safe from sickness, and we should cheer for them and learn how we can all help each other.

originalTitle: Putin says drone that crashed in Romania likely Ukrainian
title: A Flying Surprise! Drone News from Far Away!
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Hello, hello, my amazing friends all across Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with an exciting story from far, far away. We're going to talk about something called a 'drone' and a country named Romania. Don't worry, it's not scary, just a little puzzle that grown-ups are trying to solve. We love to learn about the world, don't we? So let's find out what happened and what it means. Get ready to explore!

What is a Drone? Imagine a Flying Toy!

First, let's talk about drones! Have you ever seen a remote-control toy airplane or a little helicopter that flies? Well, a drone is a bit like that! It's a machine that can fly in the sky without a pilot sitting inside it. Someone on the ground controls it with a special remote, just like you might control your toy car. Drones can be big or small. Some are tiny, like a bird, and some can be as big as a small car! People use drones for many helpful things. They can take amazing photos and videos from high up, showing us beautiful landscapes or helping to see what's happening in different places. Some drones even deliver packages, like a flying postman! But sometimes, drones can be used for more complicated things, especially when countries are having disagreements. It's like when friends argue, but on a much bigger scale. Did you know? The very first drones were invented many, many years ago, even before your grandparents were born! But they looked very different back then.

Where Did Our Drone Fall? Meet Romania!

Now, let's talk about where this drone news comes from. Our story happened in a country called Romania. Can you say "Ro-may-nee-uh"? Romania is a beautiful country in Europe, very far from Africa. It has tall mountains, deep forests, and amazing old castles that look like they're from a fairytale! Many friendly people live there. Romania is a peaceful country. People there like to live quietly and enjoy their lovely land. Imagine a place with lots of green trees and sunny days – that's a bit like Romania! Did you know? Romania is famous for a region called Transylvania, which has many old stories about princes and dragons! It's a truly magical place.

Who is Ukraine? Romania's Neighbor!

Close to Romania is another country called Ukraine. Can you say "Yoo-krayn"? Ukraine is Romania's neighbor. Think of it like two houses right next to each other on the same street. Neighbors usually get along and help each other. Right now, Ukraine is having a very big and difficult time. Another big country, Russia, and Ukraine are having a serious disagreement, which grown-ups call a 'conflict' or even a 'war.' This means that things are not peaceful there, and sometimes things can be a bit messy or even fly across borders. It's very sad when countries don't get along. Did you know? Ukraine is one of the biggest countries in Europe! It has a very rich history and beautiful sunflower fields that stretch for miles and miles.

Who is Mr. Putin?

In our news story, we hear about a person called Mr. Putin. Mr. Putin is the leader of Russia. Russia is the big country that is having a disagreement with Ukraine. Leaders of countries are like the head of a very big family. They make important decisions for their country and talk to leaders of other countries. Mr. Putin said something about the drone that fell in Romania.

What Happened Exactly? A Drone Fell from the Sky!

So, here's what happened: a drone, one of those flying machines we talked about, fell out of the sky! It crashed in Romania, in a place very close to Ukraine. This was a bit of a surprise! Imagine if a toy from your neighbor's yard suddenly landed in your garden. You'd wonder where it came from, right? When the drone fell, people from Romania went to look at it. It didn't hurt anyone, which is the most important thing! Everyone was safe, which is a big relief. Mr. Putin, the leader of Russia, then said something about this drone. He said that he thinks the drone most likely came from Ukraine. He meant that the drone might have been one used by Ukraine, and perhaps it accidentally flew too far or crashed on its own. This is what grown-ups are discussing and trying to understand.

Why is This Important? Thinking About Borders!

Why is this news important? Well, countries have what we call 'borders.' Think of a border like an invisible line on the ground that separates one country from another, just like your fence separates your yard from your neighbor's. When something from one country, like a drone, flies over or falls into another country, it's a big deal. It's like if your neighbor's toy car kept rolling into your garden without them asking. Grown-ups need to talk about it. Romania and Ukraine are neighbors. When there's trouble in Ukraine, things sometimes move across the border into Romania, even by accident. It makes everyone think about safety. Did you know? Countries often have special guards at their borders to make sure only the right people and things cross over. These are border guards!

Being Good Neighbors

This story reminds us that countries, just like people, need to be good neighbors. They need to talk to each other, especially when things are tricky. It's important for everyone to feel safe in their homes and their countries. When things like drones fall unexpectedly, it can make people worry. So, countries need to work together to keep everyone safe and peaceful. Think about how you play with your friends. You share your toys and play nicely, right? Countries try to do the same, even if it's much harder sometimes.

Staying Safe and Learning More!

So, a drone fell in Romania, and Mr. Putin said it might be from Ukraine. Grown-ups are still talking about it to understand everything. But the most important thing is that no one was hurt! And we got to learn about drones, Romania, and Ukraine! Always remember to be curious, ask questions, and learn about the wonderful world around us. There are so many amazing things to discover! Keep learning, keep shining, and stay safe, my dear friends! See you next time for more exciting news!
summary: A drone fell in Romania, and the leader of Russia, Mr. Putin, said it was likely from Ukraine, sparking discussions among countries about borders and safety.
whyMatters: When objects like drones from one country fall into another, especially when countries nearby are having a big disagreement, it makes people worry about safety and means countries need to talk to each other to sort things out.

originalTitle: US, UK and Australia to develop underwater drone technology
title: Amazing Robots Under the Sea!
content:
Hello, amazing explorers! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news all the way from across the big, big oceans! Are you ready for a story about robots? And guess what? These robots go under the water! How cool is that? Today, we are talking about special robot friends. They are like super smart fish but they are made of metal and wires. They are called underwater drones! Wow!

What Are Underwater Drones?

Imagine your favorite toy submarine. It can dive deep, right? Well, underwater drones are like super-duper real-life toy submarines, but they can do much more! They are robots. A robot is a machine that can do jobs by itself or with a little help from people. Some robots can clean your house, some can build cars, and some can even play with you! These new robots don't need a pilot inside them. People control them from far away, or they can even follow rules by themselves. They swim around, exploring the big, blue ocean. Did you know? There are also flying drones! You might have seen small ones flying in the sky. They have cameras and can take cool pictures from high up. Underwater drones are their cousins who love to swim! They are made to go where people might find it hard or dangerous to go. Deep down, where it's very dark and cold!

Who Is Making These Super Robots?

This amazing idea comes from three special countries working together! They are like a team of best friends with a big plan. The first country is the USA. That's America! It's a very big country far away. The second is the UK, which is the United Kingdom, also known as Great Britain or England. And the third is Australia, a land of kangaroos and koalas! These three countries have a special friendship club called 'Aukus'. It's like your school club, but for big countries! They promise to help each other and share clever ideas to keep everyone safe. So, USA, UK, and Australia are working as a team. They are sharing their smart ideas and best engineers to build these fantastic underwater robots. Teamwork makes the dream work, right?

Why Do We Need Robots Under The Sea?

That's a very good question! These robots have two very important jobs. Two big missions! Mission number one: Protecting special internet ropes! Yes, you heard that right. Ropes! These are not just any ropes. They are called 'undersea cables'. They are very, very long wires that lie on the bottom of the ocean. They stretch all the way from one country to another! Imagine a giant invisible string that connects your phone to your grandma's phone, even if she lives in another country! That's what these cables do. They carry all the internet signals. When you watch your favorite cartoons online, or talk to a friend far away, the message probably travels through one of these undersea cables. They are like the super highways for our internet! Did you know? Some of these cables are thicker than a garden hose! And they can be thousands of kilometers long! That's like driving across many, many countries! These cables are very important, and we need to keep them safe. Sometimes they can get damaged by big ships, or by natural things in the ocean. The underwater drones can swim along these cables, like helpful guards, making sure they are okay. If they see a problem, they can tell people quickly. This helps fix the cables fast so we don't lose our internet or phone connections. Hooray for connecting the world!

Mission Two: Keeping Our Oceans Safe!

The second big job for these underwater robots is to help keep the big blue ocean safe. This is called 'boosting naval defence'. What is a 'navy'? A navy is a country's special group of ships and sailors. They are like the protectors of the sea. Their job is to keep the waters safe for everyone. Like policemen and policewomen, but for the ocean! The navy uses big ships, submarines, and planes to do their job. Now, they will have super-smart robot friends to help them even more. These underwater drones can go on special missions. They can explore parts of the ocean that are very deep or hard to reach. They can look for anything that might cause trouble. Think of it like having a super good detective. The drones can see things very far away. They have special eyes, like cameras, and special ears, like sensors, to listen to the ocean. They can help navy ships stay safe by warning them about hidden dangers or helping them understand what's happening under the waves. This makes the navy even stronger and better at protecting our seas. Did you know? The deepest part of the ocean is called the Mariana Trench! It's so deep that if you put Mount Everest inside it, the top of the mountain would still be a mile underwater!

How Do These Robots Work?

These clever robots use amazing technology. That's a big word for smart tools and ideas! They have special engines that help them swim very quietly. They can dive very deep down into the water. And they can stay there for a long time, watching and listening. They have cameras to see all around them. They can send pictures and videos back to the people controlling them. It's like having eyes under the sea! They also have special gadgets that can 'feel' things in the water, like temperature or movement. All this information helps the people above the water understand what's happening below. They are like super explorers, charting the unknown and keeping an eye on the known.

Why Is This News Important For Us?

This is very important news because it means a safer and more connected world for everyone, even for us here in Africa! When undersea cables are safe, we can all enjoy the internet better. We can learn new things, talk to our friends, and share stories with family far away. When the oceans are safe, it's good for everyone. It helps trade, fishing, and protects the beautiful sea animals that live there. Working together, like USA, UK, and Australia are doing, is a great example for all of us. When we help each other, we can do amazing things! So, let's cheer for these new underwater robot heroes! They will be swimming deep down, helping to keep our world safe and connected. That's all for today from your friendly reporter! Keep exploring and keep learning, my little ocean adventurers!
summary: Three big countries, USA, UK, and Australia, are making special robots called underwater drones to keep important internet cables safe and make the ocean safer for everyone.
whyMatters: This is important because these robot helpers will make sure our internet works well for talking to friends and family, and they will help keep the big oceans safe for ships and sea animals everywhere.
